audit_items.proof_trace: All internal pointer dereferences (lr, ber, bisect array v) are properly guarded by explicit NULL checks. The public API functions lack assert(ld != NULL) seen in other libldap functions (ldap_unbind_ext, ldap_add_ext), and lr->lr_conn at line 262 is protected only by a debug-only assert; however, the invariant that INPROGRESS status implies non-NULL lr_conn holds for all normal request flows (request.c:362-365 sets both atomically). No external attacker can force NULL ld via the LDAP protocol, as ldap_initialize failure on memory allocation is not network-triggerable.
